title: To require disclosure when personalized algorithmic pricing is used, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This policy aims to require businesses to disclose when they use personalized algorithmic pricing to customers. Its purpose is to provide transparency and inform consumer decision-making.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act is a policy aimed at developing a workforce that trusts, understands, and utilizes AI effectively. The specifics of the policy are not mentioned in the title or provided description, so this is a basic interpretation based on the provided information.

title: To require reports regarding artificial intelligence-related job impacts, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This document requires companies to report on the impact of artificial intelligence on jobs. The policy aims to address the effects of AI on employment and work conditions.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to establish a center on artificial intelligence to ensure continued United States leadership in research, development, and evaluation of artificial intelligence systems, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to maintain US leadership in AI research and development through the establishment of a new center, focusing on improving AI systems. It plans to modify the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to achieve this goal.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to prevent employers from using automated decision systems in certain ways. It appears to be related to employment laws, particularly those concerning unfair treatment or discrimination by technology.

title: A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose an excise tax on systemically important AI activity, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill aims to introduce an excise tax on certain types of artificial intelligence activities that are deemed systemically important. The tax would be imposed to regulate and fund the development and oversight of AI systems with significant societal impact.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: This legislation, known as the Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026, aims to address the potential environmental consequences of artificial intelligence. The exact details of the Act are unknown, but it suggests a focus on mitigating potential negative effects on the environment.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 594.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 aims to regulate and utilize artificial intelligence within Small Business Administration programs. This policy likely seeks to promote the effective integration of AI into SBA operations.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 aims to ensure accountability and security in the development and use of artificial intelligence in the military. The act likely focuses on establishing guidelines and regulations to prevent AI misuse and promote transparency in military AI operations.

title: A bill to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct annual assessments on threats to the United States posed by the use of generative artificial intelligence for terrorism, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This bill requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to assess annual threats to the US from the use of generative AI for terrorism. It also addresses additional purposes related to the regulation of generative AI.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to regulate the harmful effects of Artificial Intelligence on the environment. The Act seeks to establish guidelines and standards for the development and use of AI systems to minimize their negative ecological impacts.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard individuals from fake or misleading information generated by artificial intelligence. The law likely establishes regulations to prevent AI systems from deceiving consumers and ensures that AI outputs are accurate and trustworthy.

title: To promote United States leadership in technical standards by directing the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Department of State to take certain actions to encourage and enable United States participation in developing standards and specifications for artificial intelligence and other critical and emerging technologies, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to boost the US's influence in global technical standards. It directs the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Department of State to encourage US participation in developing standards for emerging technologies like AI.

title: To establish the Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This policy establishes a Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence to oversee and manage AI-related matters. The department will likely focus on advancing AI research, development, and regulation.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The "Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act" aims to safeguard consumers from being misled or deceived by artificial intelligence systems. The policy likely proposes regulations to prevent the use of AI in deceptive or fraudulent practices.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act is a policy aimed at utilizing artificial intelligence to assess and track heat emergency situations. The policy likely seeks to leverage AI technology to better respond to and mitigate the effects of severe heat-related events.

title: To provide for a right of action against Federal employees for violations of First Amendment rights relating to the use or development of artificial intelligence.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy document aims to give individuals the right to take action against Federal employees who violate their First Amendment rights involving artificial intelligence. It seeks to protect people's freedom of speech and expression in relation to AI.

title: To require the Secretary of Energy to establish a centralized resource for access to data to facilitate biological research through enabling advanced computational methods such as artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to require the creation of a centralized resource for accessing data to facilitate biological research by using advanced computational methods like artificial intelligence. The goal of this policy is to support more efficient and effective biological research for various purposes.

title: A bill to require the Secretary of Energy to establish a centralized resource for access to data to facilitate biological research through enabling advanced computational methods such as artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
summary: The document is a bill that aims to set up a centralized database to make biological research data easily accessible. This data would be used to develop advanced technologies like artificial intelligence that can aid in research.

title: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives with respect to the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services and housing industries.
action: Placed on the House Calendar, Calendar No. 67.
summary: This document expresses the U.S. House of Representatives' view on using artificial intelligence in the financial services and housing industries. It likely addresses concerns, benefits, or regulations regarding AI's impact on these sectors.

title: Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: The Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act aims to support the growth and development of small businesses in the artificial intelligence sector. The exact details of the policy are unclear due to lack of description.

title: A bill to require the Office of Financial Research to compel data relating to the financing of artificial intelligence development, provide that data to Congress, and issue recommendations to financial regulatory agencies and Congress to mitigate financial stability risk, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
summary: This bill aims to collect data on the financing of artificial intelligence development, share it with Congress, and issue recommendations to address potential risks to financial stability. The goal is to provide Congress and financial regulatory agencies with information and guidance to mitigate threats to the US financial system.

title: Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.
summary: This policy, titled "Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act," likely aims to prevent the use of artificial intelligence to manipulate or deceive voters in elections. The specific measures and guidelines of the policy are not clear due to a lack of detailed description.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at regulating the use of Artificial Intelligence in farming. The purpose of the act is likely to ensure food safety, sustainability, and fair trade in the agricultural industry.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act aims to provide support and empowerment to small AI innovators, likely through policy changes or funding.

title: A bill to amend title 10, United States Code, to establish a policy for the Department of Defense on maximizing autonomy and artificial intelligence systems, to establish requirements relating to Department review and verification of autonomous weapon systems, and artificial intelligence capabilities, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: A bill proposes changes to US military code to regulate the use of artificial intelligence and autonomous weapon systems. It aims to establish guidelines and oversight for the Department of Defense on maximizing AI capabilities and reviewing autonomous systems.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: The Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026 aims to protect American artificial intelligence (AI) models from theft and unauthorized use. This legislation likely seeks to establish guidelines and penalties for individuals or organizations found guilty of stealing or mishandling U.S. AI models.

title: NSF AI Education Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The NSF AI Education Act of 2025 is a proposed policy aimed at advancing education in the field of artificial intelligence research and development. The policy likely focuses on creating initiatives and funding opportunities to support AI education and research, possibly through the National Science Foundation (NSF).
